# Dev-Dash Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `devdash prime` after compaction, clear, or new session.
> Use `devdash` (dev-dash CLI) for ALL task tracking — never `bd`.

**Project**: dev-dash (jasonmassey/dev-dash)  |  **ID**: `896b3dbc`
**Health**: 165 open, 0 in progress, 32 blocked

## All Projects

| Short | Full UUID | Name | Repo |
|-------|-----------|------|------|
| `896b3dbc` | `896b3dbc-65a8-4ff8-82a3-b8a6a43e64b8` | dev-dash | (jasonmassey/dev-dash) |
| `672ac04b` | `672ac04b-0e69-413a-b3de-8909618aa277` | devdash-feedback-james | (no repo) |
| `47eb046a` | `47eb046a-b02a-41b4-926f-8bc7138ab470` | devdash-cli-go | (jasonmassey/devdash-cli-go) |
| `8d91343a` | `8d91343a-927a-4753-9cec-3a4ccb6360fe` | devdash-mcp | (no repo) |
| `639a7591` | `639a7591-d14a-4229-9556-315fecf5094e` | devdash-pink | (jasonmassey/devdash-variant-pink) |
| `95ca3de0` | `95ca3de0-7e4f-4f9e-9b17-36f5609cfa11` | devdash-cli | (jasonmassey/devdash-cli) |
| `e4c6b30d` | `e4c6b30d-4d24-4529-a559-b8e131f5cb51` | devdash-green | (jasonmassey/devdash-variant-green) |
| `f7730420` | `f7730420-9537-4e90-88ae-353638720b90` | devdash-blue | (jasonmassey/devdash-variant-blue) |
| `0d7af93a` | `0d7af93a-74d8-4797-899e-7a0689d993a2` | devdash-yellow | (jasonmassey/dd-yellow) |
| `66804464` | `66804464-9230-4d36-9c55-89d5bb778839` | dd-yellow | (jasonmassey/dd-yellow) |
| `0e58e38f` | `0e58e38f-9aeb-48d7-982a-c084c8f5acb7` | writegoodcomments | (jasonmassey/writegoodcomments) |
| `bd575735` | `bd575735-20cc-4171-8355-b7aef531f008` | deedee-working-proto | (no repo) |
| `ce80b310` | `ce80b310-e3e6-4172-b997-19f3943027ad` | DD Stress Test | (no repo) |
| `18b82716` | `18b82716-4556-490c-afd2-3245bbea86ab` | dd-mail | (no repo) |
| `071ef5cc` | `071ef5cc-0f6d-449a-ad55-a460cde38dcd` | shared email | (no repo) |
| `aebcb113` | `aebcb113-6c7b-46d5-b50e-5d401cb9bae7` | dd-landing-page | (no repo) |
| `abe3a75b` | `abe3a75b-aa75-43b4-8463-89ca48df5b2e` | MatrixProgressBar | (masseymoproductions-inc/MatrixProgressBar) |
| `8114ba71` | `8114ba71-8967-4338-be71-bfdc763e48a0` | flexible | (jasonmassey/flexible) |
| `a2b988de` | `a2b988de-010a-4f25-b0dd-9d4c453ebc0a` | python-random-quote | (jasonmassey/python-random-quote) |
| `f3040821` | `f3040821-1571-4838-84b3-e6c9f20bb7ba` | tic-tac-toe | (jasonmassey/tic-tac-toe) |
| `22621462` | `22621462-4df1-4076-baeb-bd2448b60688` | dd-red | (jasonmassey/dd-red) |
| `207cfedb` | `207cfedb-c0b3-46c4-a897-7518ae0a3e29` | dd-green | (jasonmassey/dd-green) |
| `66d43bbc` | `66d43bbc-8f14-45ff-87b1-91dff7093829` | devdash-green | (jasonmassey/devdash-variant-green) |
| `182e8ce9` | `182e8ce9-9d78-4bc2-8623-518ecedee507` | dd-mail-gas | (no repo) |

Use `DD_PROJECT_ID=<full-uuid> devdash <command>` to target a specific project.
Short prefixes work for project IDs too: `DD_PROJECT_ID=47eb046a devdash ready`

## Rules (MANDATORY)
- **Issue-first**: Create a devdash issue BEFORE writing code. No exceptions.
- **Issue-per-commit**: Every git commit must map to a devdash issue. If scope expands, create new issues.
- **Mark in-progress**: `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress` before starting work.
- **Pre-commit checkpoint**: Before each `git commit`, verify you have an issue. If not, create one.
- **Close after push**: Only close issues after `git push` succeeds — never before.
- **No orphaned work**: At session end, every commit must map to a closed issue.
- **Path**: `/Users/jason/.local/bin/devdash`
- **Preserve stderr**: Avoid `2>/dev/null` on devdash commands — stderr contains error details you'll need for debugging failures.
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use `bd`, TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking

## Quick Reference
- **Start (task already named)**: `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`
- **Start (need a task)**: `devdash ready` → `devdash show <id>` → `devdash update <id> --status=in_progress`
- **Complete**: git add → commit → push → `devdash close <id> --summary="..." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)`
- **Report**: `devdash report <id> --status=code_complete|committed|pushed|error --summary="..."`
- Close summaries are institutional memory — include what, why, decisions, surprises, follow-ups.
- One issue per commit. Scope creep = new issue. Multi-step = parent + children.

## Output Formats
- There is no `--json` flag. Do not try `--json`, `--format`, or `--output`.
- These commands already return JSON: `show`, `find`, `activity`, `comments`, `jobs show`, `score <id>`, `token list`, `sync`
- All other commands return human-readable text. Parse UUIDs from `create`/`close`/`update` output directly.

## On-Demand Reference
Run these when you need detailed guidance:
- `devdash help cli` — Full command reference (flags, ID formats, --since syntax)
- `devdash help workflow` — When to create issues, decomposition patterns, bead relationships
- `devdash help close` — Close summary expectations with examples
- `devdash help pr` — PR footer format and multi-issue PRs
- `devdash help projects` — Cross-project dependencies and multi-repo work
- `devdash help report` — Progress reporting cadence and status values

message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

I want to fix two things about the left nav. First, let's replace the direct link to the survey tab with a direct link to the board tab, and update the icon and label in the left nav accordingly. Second, when I'm in a project, both the tasks and survey are highlighted in the left nav. Only one should be, when it's the current tab open. Let's fix these in tandem. scope it out

- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the …
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/…
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v…

content.text: Let me explore the left nav and routing to understand the current implementation.

input.prompt:
I need to understand the left navigation sidebar in this React app. Specifically:

1. Find the left nav / sidebar component that shows navigation links (tasks, survey, board, etc.)
2. How are nav items defined — icons, labels, routes/paths?
3. How is the "active" or "highlighted" state determined for each nav item?
4. What routes exist for project tabs (tasks, survey, board)?

Look in the `client/src` directory. Search for sidebar, nav, left-nav, sidenav, navigation components. Also look at the router configuration to understand project tab routes.

Report file paths, line numbers, and relevant code snippets for all findings. Be thorough — I need to understand both the nav item definitions and the active-state logic.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Summary: Left Navigation Sidebar in Dev-Dash

I've thoroughly analyzed the React app's left navigation sidebar. Here are the findings:

### 1. **Sidebar Component Location**
**File:** `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/dev-dash/client/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` (lines 1-160)

### 2. **Navigation Item Definition**

The sidebar uses a `NavigationItem` interface defined at lines 21-27:

```typescript
interface NavigationItem {
  name: string;
  href: string | ((projectId?: string) => string);
  icon: React.ComponentType<{ className?: string }>;
  indent?: boolean;
  end?: boolean;
}
```

**Navigation items are defined at lines 29-39:**

| Name | Icon | Route/Href | Indent | End | Notes |
|------|------|-----------|--------|-----|-------|
| Projects | FolderKanban | `/dashboard` | - | true | Root dashboard |
| Tasks | ListTodo | `/project/{projectId}?tab=beads` | yes | - | Dynamic, project-aware |
| Survey | Target | `/project/{projectId}?tab=survey` | yes | - | Dynamic, project-aware |
| Jobs | Briefcase | `/jobs` | - | - | Global jobs page |
| Notifications | Bell | `/notifications` | - | - | Global notifications |
| Settings | Settings | `/settings` | - | - | Global settings |
| Products | Palette | `/products` | - | - | Global products |
| UI Lab | FlaskConical | `/lab` | - | - | UI component lab |
| Mobile | Smartphone | `/m` | - | - | Mobile experience |

**Key details:**
- Tasks and Survey items are **dynamic**: their `href` is a function that accepts an optional `projectId`
- The `indent: true` property causes sub-items to be indented when the sidebar is expanded (line 121)
- The `end: true` flag on "Projects" ensures it's only active when exactly matching `/dashboard` (not sub-routes)
- Task-related items automatically default to `/dashboard` if no project ID is available (line 31-32)

### 3. **Project-Aware Routing Logic**
**Lines 66-77 in Sidebar.tsx:**

The component extracts the current project ID from the URL pathname using a regex:
```typescript
const projectMatch = location.pathname.match(/^\/project\/([^/?]+)/);
const currentProjectId = projectMatch?.[1];
```

It then stores the last visited project in localStorage:
```typescript
const LAST_PROJECT_KEY = 'devdash-last-project-id';
React.useEffect(() => {
  if (currentProjectId) {
    localStorage.setItem(LAST_PROJECT_KEY, currentProjectId);
  }
}, [currentProjectId]);

const resolvedProjectId = currentProjectId || localStorage.getItem(LAST_PROJECT_KEY) || undefined;
```

This allows the Tasks/Survey navigation links to work even when you're not on a project page.

### 4. **Active/Highlighted State Logic**
**Lines 112-140 in Sidebar.tsx:**

Active state uses React Router's `NavLink` component with a callback className:

```typescript
<NavLink
  key={item.name}
  to={href}
  end={item.end}
  title={isCollapsed ? item.name : ''}
  className={({ isActive }) =>
    clsx(
      'flex items-center h-10 text-sm font-medium relative group transition-colors',
      item.indent && !isCollapsed && 'pl-4',
      isActive
        ? 'bg-brand-primary/10 text-brand-primary'     // Active styles
        : 'text-theme-muted hover:text-theme-text hover:bg-theme-border/50'
    )
  }
>
```

**Active state styling (lines 122-124):**
- **Active:** Background color `bg-brand-primary/10` + text color `text-brand-primary`
- **Inactive:** Text color `text-theme-muted` with hover effects

**How NavLink determines active state:**
- The `isActive` prop is provided by React Router's NavLink
- It compares the current location pathname against the `to` prop
- The `end={item.end}` prop controls whether child routes count as active (only used for "Projects" item)
- Query parameters in the href don't prevent matching — NavLink matches based on pathname only

**Important note:** Query parameters like `?tab=beads` are included in the href but don't affect NavLink's active matching. The pathname `/project/{projectId}` is what triggers the active state.

### 5. **Project Tab Routes**
**File:** `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/dev-dash/client/src/pages/Project.tsx`

**Main router configuration:**
**File:** `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/dev-dash/client/src/App.tsx` (line 101)
```typescript
<Route path="/project/:id" element={<Project />} />
```

**Project internal tabs are defined at lines 52 & 99-110 in Project.tsx:**

The Project component supports 8 tabs accessed via the `?tab=` query parameter:

| Tab ID | Label | Icon | Route | Conditional |
|--------|-------|------|-------|-------------|
| beads | Tasks | ListTodo | `/project/:id?tab=beads` | Always shown |
| board | Board | LayoutGrid | `/project/:id?tab=board` | Always shown |
| survey | Survey | Layers | `/project/:id?tab=survey` | Always shown |
| activity | Activity | Activity | `/project/:id?tab=activity` | Always shown |
| dispatch | Dispatch | Cpu | `/project/:id?tab=dispatch` | Only if `project.githubRepo` exists |
| agents | Agents | Bot | `/project/:id?tab=agents` | Only if `project.githubRepo` exists |
| memory | Memory | Brain | `/project/:id?tab=memory` | Always shown |
| settings | Settings | Settings | `/project/:id?tab=settings` | Always shown |

**Tab activation (lines 60-65, 69-73):**
- Reads the `tab` query parameter from URL: `const tabParam = searchParams.get('tab') as Tab | null;`
- Validates against allowed tabs: `const validTabs: Tab[] = ['dispatch', 'agents', 'board', 'beads', 'survey', 'activity', 'memory', 'settings'];`
- Defaults to `beads` if no valid tab is specified
- Syncs with useEffect to update state when URL query parameter changes

**Tab rendering (lines 177-196):**
Each tab button sets the active tab on click and displays styling based on `activeTab === tab.id` comparison.

### 6. **Sidebar Collapse/Expand Logic**
**File:** `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/dev-dash/client/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` (lines 10-23)

Sidebar collapse state is managed by AppShell and persisted to localStorage:
- Key: `'devdash-sidebar-collapsed'`
- Default: `true` (collapsed on first load)
- When collapsed: width is `w-16` (line 85 in Sidebar.tsx)
- When expanded: width is `w-44` (line 85 in Sidebar.tsx)

**Tooltip behavior (lines 135-139 in Sidebar.tsx):**
When collapsed, hovering over nav items shows a tooltip with the item's name.

### 7. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `/client/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` | Main sidebar component & nav items definition | 21-160 |
| `/client/src/pages/Project.tsx` | Project page with internal tabs | 52-110, 177-196 |
| `/client/src/App.tsx` | Route configuration | 84-120 |
| `/client/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` | Sidebar collapse state management | 10-23 |

This architecture cleanly separates the global left sidebar navigation from the project-specific tab navigation, with the sidebar intelligently using the current or last-visited project ID to generate dynamic links to specific project tabs.

### 4. **Active/Highlighted State Logic**
**Lines 112-140 in Sidebar.tsx:**

Active state uses React Router's `NavLink` component with a callback className:

```typescript
<NavLink
  key={item.name}
  to={href}
  end={item.end}
  title={isCollapsed ? item.name : ''}
  className={({ isActive }) =>
    clsx(
      'flex items-center h-10 text-sm font-medium relative group transition-colors',
      item.indent && !isCollapsed && 'pl-4',
      isActive
        ? 'bg-brand-primary/10 text-brand-primary'     // Active styles
        : 'text-theme-muted hover:text-theme-text hover:bg-theme-border/50'
    )
  }
>
```

**Active state styling (lines 122-124):**
- **Active:** Background color `bg-brand-primary/10` + text color `text-brand-primary`
- **Inactive:** Text color `text-theme-muted` with hover effects

**How NavLink determines active state:**
- The `isActive` prop is provided by React Router's NavLink
- It compares the current location pathname against the `to` prop
- The `end={item.end}` prop controls whether child routes count as active (only used for "Projects" item)
- Query parameters in the href don't prevent matching — NavLink matches based on pathname only

**Important note:** Query parameters like `?tab=beads` are included in the href but don't affect NavLink's active matching. The pathname `/project/{projectId}` is what triggers the active state.

### 5. **Project Tab Routes**
**File:** `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/dev-dash/client/src/pages/Project.tsx`

**Main router configuration:**
**File:** `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/dev-dash/client/src/App.tsx` (line 101)
```typescript
<Route path="/project/:id" element={<Project />} />
```

**Project internal tabs are defined at lines 52 & 99-110 in Project.tsx:**

The Project component supports 8 tabs accessed via the `?tab=` query parameter:

| Tab ID | Label | Icon | Route | Conditional |
|--------|-------|------|-------|-------------|
| beads | Tasks | ListTodo | `/project/:id?tab=beads` | Always shown |
| board | Board | LayoutGrid | `/project/:id?tab=board` | Always shown |
| survey | Survey | Layers | `/project/:id?tab=survey` | Always shown |
| activity | Activity | Activity | `/project/:id?tab=activity` | Always shown |
| dispatch | Dispatch | Cpu | `/project/:id?tab=dispatch` | Only if `project.githubRepo` exists |
| agents | Agents | Bot | `/project/:id?tab=agents` | Only if `project.githubRepo` exists |
| memory | Memory | Brain | `/project/:id?tab=memory` | Always shown |
| settings | Settings | Settings | `/project/:id?tab=settings` | Always shown |

**Tab activation (lines 60-65, 69-73):**
- Reads the `tab` query parameter from URL: `const tabParam = searchParams.get('tab') as Tab | null;`
- Validates against allowed tabs: `const validTabs: Tab[] = ['dispatch', 'agents', 'board', 'beads', 'survey', 'activity', 'memory', 'settings'];`
- Defaults to `beads` if no valid tab is specified
- Syncs with useEffect to update state when URL query parameter changes

**Tab rendering (lines 177-196):**
Each tab button sets the active tab on click and displays styling based on `activeTab === tab.id` comparison.

### 6. **Sidebar Collapse/Expand Logic**
**File:** `/Users/jason/playground/claudespace/dev-dash/client/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` (lines 10-23)

Sidebar collapse state is managed by AppShell and persisted to localStorage:
- Key: `'devdash-sidebar-collapsed'`
- Default: `true` (collapsed on first load)
- When collapsed: width is `w-16` (line 85 in Sidebar.tsx)
- When expanded: width is `w-44` (line 85 in Sidebar.tsx)

**Tooltip behavior (lines 135-139 in Sidebar.tsx):**
When collapsed, hovering over nav items shows a tooltip with the item's name.

### 7. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `/client/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` | Main sidebar component & nav items definition | 21-160 |
| `/client/src/pages/Project.tsx` | Project page with internal tabs | 52-110, 177-196 |
| `/client/src/App.tsx` | Route configuration | 84-120 |
| `/client/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` | Sidebar collapse state management | 10-23 |

This architecture cleanly separates the global left sidebar navigation from the project-specific tab navigation, with the sidebar intelligently using the current or last-visited project ID to generate dynamic links to specific project tabs.
Now let me read the exact code to confirm the details and understand the active-state bug.

I have a clear picture of both issues. Let me write the plan.

**Root cause of the dual-highlight bug**: Both "Tasks" and "Survey" (soon "Board") NavLinks resolve to the same pathname `/project/{projectId}` — they only differ by query parameter (`?tab=beads` vs `?tab=survey`). React Router's `NavLink` determines `isActive` based on **pathname only**, ignoring query params. So both light up whenever you're on any project page.

input.content:
# Fix Left Nav: Board Link + Active State

## Context
Two issues in the sidebar (`client/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`):
1. The "Survey" shortcut in the left nav should be replaced with a "Board" shortcut (different icon, label, and `?tab=board`).
2. Both "Tasks" and "Survey" (soon "Board") highlight simultaneously when on a project page, because `NavLink` matches on pathname only — both resolve to `/project/:id`, differing only by query param.

## Changes — single file: `Sidebar.tsx`

### 1. Replace Survey nav item with Board (line 32)
- **Label**: `Survey` → `Board`
- **Icon**: `Target` → `LayoutGrid` (already used in `Project.tsx` for the board tab)
- **href**: `?tab=survey` → `?tab=board`
- Update the `lucide-react` import: drop `Target`, add `LayoutGrid`

### 2. Fix active-state logic (lines 112–126)
Switch from `NavLink` to plain `Link` (or use `NavLink` with a custom `className` callback that overrides `isActive`). The cleanest approach: keep `NavLink` but override the active detection for items whose href is a function (the project-tab items) by checking the current `tab` query param.

Concrete logic inside the `navigation.map()`:
```tsx
const isProjectTabItem = typeof item.href === 'function';
// For project-tab items, active only when pathname matches AND tab param matches
const tabParam = new URLSearchParams(location.search).get('tab') || 'beads';
const hrefUrl = new URL(href, window.location.origin);
const itemTab = hrefUrl.searchParams.get('tab');
const isManuallyActive = isProjectTabItem
  ? location.pathname === hrefUrl.pathname && tabParam === itemTab
  : undefined; // let NavLink handle non-project items normally
```

Then in the `className` callback:
```tsx
className={({ isActive: routerActive }) => {
  const active = isManuallyActive ?? routerActive;
  return clsx(
    'flex items-center h-10 text-sm font-medium relative group transition-colors',
    item.indent && !isCollapsed && 'pl-4',
    active
      ? 'bg-brand-primary/10 text-brand-primary'
      : 'text-theme-muted hover:text-theme-text hover:bg-theme-border/50'
  );
}}
```

Parse `location.search` once before the `.map()` to avoid repeated work.

## Files to modify
- `client/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` — only file

## Verification
1. `npx tsc --noEmit` from `server/` (type check)
2. Start dev server, open a project
3. Click Tasks tab — only Tasks highlights in left nav
4. Click Board tab — only Board highlights in left nav
5. Navigate to Jobs, Settings, etc. — neither Tasks nor Board highlights
6. Collapsed sidebar: tooltip says "Board" (not "Survey"), icon is grid

content.text:
Here's the summary:

**Issue 1 — Survey → Board**: Swap the nav item's label, icon (`Target` → `LayoutGrid`), and query param (`?tab=survey` → `?tab=board`). One-line change plus an import tweak.

**Issue 2 — Dual highlight bug**: `NavLink` matches on pathname only, so both "Tasks" and "Board" resolve to `/project/:id` and both light up. Fix: override the active detection for project-tab items by also comparing the `?tab` query param from the current URL against the item's target tab.

Single file change: `Sidebar.tsx`.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Replace Survey with Board in left nav + fix dual-highlight" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Replace the "Survey" shortcut in the left nav with "Board" (new icon, label, `?tab=board`)
- Fix bug where both Tasks and Survey/Board highlighted simultaneously — NavLink only matches on pathname, so both project-tab items lit up. Now overrides active detection to also compare the `?tab` query param.

## Test plan
- [ ] Open a project, click Tasks — only Tasks highlights
- [ ] Click Board — only Board highlights
- [ ] Navigate to Jobs/Settings — neither Tasks nor Board highlights
- [ ] Collapsed sidebar shows correct "Board" tooltip and grid icon

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- devdash:a0b42bc7-39d4-4774-af37-e56edd6b4055 -->
EOF
)"
input.description: Create PR for the sidebar fix

Fixed dual-highlight bug where both Tasks and Board lit up simultaneously — NavLink only matches pathname, so overrode active detection for project-tab items to also compare the ?tab query param." --commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
